title: Daily Market Digest: March 25, 2026
subtitle: Market closes lower amid geopolitical uncertainty, key economic data released

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Close Lower as Geopolitical Tensions Reignite Inflation Concerns
items.summary: U.S. equities finished the day in negative territory, reversing earlier gains, as renewed concerns over rising oil prices and persistent Middle East conflict weighed on investor sentiment. While initial hopes of a U.S.-Iran peace plan provided an early boost, Iran's denial of direct talks led to market uncertainty, with all major indices closing down. Economic data released today showed an increase in U.S. import and export prices for February.

items.headline: Arm Holdings Soars on AI Chip Announcement
items.summary: Arm Holdings ($ARM) saw a significant surge, rising 20% after unveiling its inaugural native AI data center chip, boosting the broader technology sector's sentiment for AI innovation.

items.headline: Sarepta Therapeutics Rises on Trial Data
items.summary: Sarepta ($SRPT) advanced 23.17% following the release of early trial data for two of its rare disease therapies.

items.headline: Battalion Oil Plummets
items.summary: Battalion Oil ($BATL) experienced a sharp decline, falling 42.1% to $5.29, making it a leading loser for the day.

items.headline: Salesforce Drops on Market Uncertainty
items.summary: Salesforce, Inc. ($CRM) was a major drag on the Dow, with its stock price falling 6.2% amidst the broader market uncertainty.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads, Tech and Communication Services Lag
items.summary: Seven out of 11 broad sectors ended in positive territory, with the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) rising 2.1%. Conversely, Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ($XLC) fell 2.5%, Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ($XLRE) declined 0.8%, and Information Technology Select Sector SPDR ($XLK) was down 0.7%.

items.headline: U.S. Import and Export Prices Rise in February
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that U.S. import prices increased by 1.3% in February, following a 0.6% advance in January. U.S. export prices also rose by 1.5% in February, after a 0.6% increase in the prior month. No other key economic reports were scheduled for today.

items.headline: Mixed Bag of Earnings: Chewy Misses, Others See Varied Results
items.summary: Several major companies reported earnings today. Chewy ($CHWY) missed Zacks Consensus Estimate with earnings of $0.27 per share, leading to a 0.59% decline in its stock price. Beyond Meat ($BYND) was down 4.61% and Paychex ($PAYX) declined 3.09%. However, PDD Holdings ($PDD) gained 1.91% and Jefferies ($JEF) rose 2.53%. Enlivex Ltd. ($ENLV) also announced its full-year 2025 results, reporting a net income of $1.23 billion.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Market Volatility and Oil Prices
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East continued to be a significant factor in market volatility. While early trading saw a rally on hopes of a U.S.-Iran peace plan, these gains reversed after Iran denied direct negotiations. Rising oil prices, with Brent crude around $98.92-$104.5 per barrel and WTI crude at $87.90-$92.4 per barrel, contributed to inflation concerns. The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield, however, fell near 4.33%.
